title: Decision of CJI on Protective measures
text:

Decision on Protective measures
C„riminal C„ase „File _Dossier penal
Ordonnance relative aux mesures de protection
tIU8 No 002 14 08 2006
6R5§S 5S5fi55 Iirvestigation Instruction
tfU8 No 001 18 07 2007
We You Bunleng and Marcel Lemonde Co Investigating Judges ofthe Extraordinary Chambers in
the Courts ofCambodia
Having seenthe Law on the Establishment ofthe Extraordinary Chambers dated October 27 2004
Having seen Rules 29 and 55 ofthe Internal Rules ofthe Extraordinary Chambers
Having seenthejudicial investigation opened against KAING GUECKEAV alias DUCH
Having seen the request for protective measures submitted by the Co Prosecutors within the
Introductory Submission dated July 18 2007 paragraphs 119 120 121 and 125
REASONS FOR THE DECISION
The request demands that protective measures be ordered with respect to victims witnesses andthird
persons who are referred to either directly or by necessary implication in this Introductory
Submission the accompanying case file and any other material provided therewith The Co
Prosecutors consider that these measures should include in particular the prohibition of defence
access to the list of potential witnesses until such time as a comprehensive specific individual
assessment is undertaken
The request does not specify the names of the individuals concerned nor how the failure to provide
protective measures would endanger the lives and security of the hundreds of victims witnesses or
thirdpersons mentioned in the Introductory Submission and in its annexes 14000 pages Moreover
it does not explain the reasons why an analysis ofthe real risks entailed by some ofthese individuals
was not done during the long preliminary inquiry preceding the opening ofthejudicial investigation
It is clear that the Co Investigating Judges must take all appropriate measures to ensure the protection
ofwitnesses and victims However they must not do so indiscriminately that is to say they cannot
do so without a clear justification and for an unlimited number of individuals They must also
guarantee the rights ofthe defence which are fundamental rights in a criminal trial and which include

in particular the right of the lawyers for the Charged Person to have access to the case file and
annexed documents
Given these considerations the request as drafted cannot be accepted However in the current
absence ofany effective measures and during the period needed to consult the units responsible for the
protection of witnesses and victims in order to determine whether it may be necessary to order
protective measures for certain victims and witnesses the delivery of copies of the case file to the
lawyers for the parties must be denied In addition they must be reminded that they are bound by
professional secrecy and must not divulge the contents ofany information obtained from consulting the
case file to any person in any manner whatsoever in particular the names and addresses of the
witnesses under penalty ofdisciplinary and criminal sanctions
FOR THESE REASONS
1 Partially deny the request forprotective measures dated July 18 2007
2 Allow the lawyers for the Charged Person to consult the entire case file and its annexed
documents for the preparation ofhis defence
3 Prohibit provisionally and until completion ofa detailed evaluation ofthe risk incurred by all
individuals mentioned in the Introductory Submission and the annexed documents the delivery
ofcopies ofthe case file to the lawyers forthe parties
4 Recall that everyone who has access to the case file is prohibited from disclosing publicly in
any manner whatsoever any information obtained through consultation of the case file
especially the names and relevant information that would allow the identification and location
ofthe witnesses under penalty ofdisciplinary or criminal sanctions in accordance with Rules
35 and 38 ofthe Internal Rules ofthe Extraordinary Chambers
5 State that for the purposes ofthis order the term public means any physical person or legal
entity government organization entity group and media and that it includes in particular the
family friends and relatives of the Charged Person moreover the term case file and
annexed documents means any information statements minutes reports photos or other
items submitted whether in paper or electronic format
6 State that the present order does not preclude any party or person from requesting in
accordance with Rule 29 of the Internal Rules any protective measures they deem to be
necessary but that in support ofsuch request they must specify
a the identity of the witnesses victims or third persons who would benefit from these
measures and
b how in the absence of the measures the lives and health of these witnesses victims
their families ortheir kin would be placed in grave danger
This order was drafted in Khmer and French and subsequently translated into English
Done in Phnom Penh July 30 2007
Co Investigating Judges
